articles.headline: India-Africa Summit Concludes, Unveils Delhi Declaration on Strategic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The third India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS-III) concluded in New Delhi, adopting the 'Delhi Declaration' and a comprehensive 'Framework for Strategic Cooperation'.
• Key areas of collaboration include enhanced trade and investment, defence and security, climate change, digital transformation, and capacity building.
• India pledged an additional $12 billion in concessional credit to African nations over the next five years, focusing on infrastructure and sustainable development projects.
• The declaration emphasizes a shared vision for a multipolar world order, advocating for reforms in global institutions, including the UN Security Council.
• Leaders discussed joint efforts to combat terrorism, maritime piracy, and promote food and energy security across the continent.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: IAFS-III held in New Delhi.
Adopted 'Delhi Declaration' and 'Framework for Strategic Cooperation'.
India pledged $12 billion in concessional credit to Africa.
Focus on infrastructure, sustainable development, climate change, digital transformation.
Advocacy for UN Security Council reforms.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Delhi Declaration in strengthening India-Africa relations and its implications for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss how India's engagement with African nations aligns with its 'Global South' leadership aspirations and counter-balancing geopolitical influences.
Evaluate the potential economic and strategic benefits for both India and African countries through enhanced cooperation in defence, trade, and technology transfer.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and various African nations shaking hands and posing for a group photograph at the India-Africa Forum Summit.
articles.trivia: The first India-Africa Forum Summit was held in 2008, marking a significant step in India's structured engagement with the African continent.

articles.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the benchmark repo rate at 6.50% in its latest review.
• This decision comes despite calls for a potential rate cut, as the MPC cited persistent inflationary concerns, especially from food prices and global crude oil volatility.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4% while supporting growth.
• Economic growth projections remain robust, but the central bank highlighted risks from geopolitical tensions and uneven monsoon distribution.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed, with the RBI indicating readiness to use various tools to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
articles.source: Reserve Bank of India / Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.50%.
Decision by Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Primary reason: persistent inflation, particularly food prices and crude oil.
RBI's medium-term inflation target is 4%.
Geopolitical tensions and monsoon distribution cited as risks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, especially in the context of global uncertainties.
Discuss the effectiveness of the current monetary policy stance in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures and its impact on various sectors of the economy.
Examine the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy to achieve macroeconomic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Adjourns Hearing on Review Petitions for Electoral Bonds Scheme
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India adjourned the hearing on review petitions challenging its earlier judgment on the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Petitioners are seeking a re-examination of the constitutional validity of the scheme, citing concerns over transparency and potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• The earlier judgment had struck down the scheme, but the government had sought a review, arguing its importance for curbing black money in political funding.
• The court indicated that a larger bench might be constituted to address the complex constitutional questions involved.
• The outcome of these petitions will have significant implications for political funding and electoral transparency in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court adjourned hearing on Electoral Bonds review petitions.
Petitioners challenge constitutional validity, citing transparency concerns.
Government previously argued scheme curbs black money.
Potential for a larger bench to be constituted.
Impacts political funding and electoral transparency.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme in the context of electoral transparency and political funding reforms in India.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles related to free and fair elections, particularly concerning financial aspects of political parties.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for political funding that can ensure transparency, accountability, and reduce the influence of corporate donations.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The Electoral Bonds scheme was introduced in 2017 through a Finance Bill, amending several acts including the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Test-Fires Next-Generation Semi-Cryogenic Engine
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful long-duration hot test of its new semi-cryogenic engine, powering future heavy-lift rockets.
• This engine, designed for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) missions and future Gaganyaan variants, uses a combination of liquid oxygen and refined kerosene (ISROSENE) as propellants.
• The test validates critical components and systems, moving India closer to developing more cost-effective and powerful launch capabilities.
• The indigenous development of such advanced propulsion systems is crucial for India's ambitions in human spaceflight and deep-space missions.
• This achievement reduces India's reliance on foreign technology and enhances its competitive edge in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully test-fired a new semi-cryogenic engine.
Engine uses liquid oxygen and ISROSENE (refined kerosene).
Designed for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) and Gaganyaan variants.
Aims for cost-effective and powerful launch capabilities.
Boosts India's human spaceflight and deep-space mission ambitions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous development of advanced propulsion systems like the semi-cryogenic engine for India's space program and national security.
Analyze how the development of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology can revolutionize space access and contribute to India's economic growth in the space sector.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in space technology, considering its current advancements and future aspirations.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of ISRO's new semi-cryogenic engine undergoing a hot test.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first successful test of a cryogenic engine for its GSLV Mk-II rocket was in 2014, making India one of the few nations with this complex technology.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched a comprehensive 'National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management 2025-30'.
• The plan aims to drastically reduce waste generation, maximize resource utilization, and promote recycling and reuse across various sectors.
• It focuses on developing robust infrastructure for waste segregation, collection, processing, and disposal, with an emphasis on urban and industrial waste.
• Key initiatives include policy incentives for industries adopting circular economy principles, public awareness campaigns, and technological innovation in waste treatment.
• The plan is aligned with India's commitments to sustainable development goals and climate change mitigation, targeting a significant reduction in landfill burden.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / PIB
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Environment launched 'National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management 2025-30'.
Aims to reduce waste, maximize resource utilization, promote recycling/reuse.
Focus on urban and industrial waste infrastructure.
Includes policy incentives and public awareness campaigns.
Aligned with SDGs and climate change mitigation.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of a circular economy and its importance in addressing India's growing waste management challenges.
Analyze the key features and potential impacts of the National Action Plan on waste management, considering its economic, social, and environmental dimensions.
Discuss the challenges in implementing circular economy principles in India, particularly regarding behavioral change, technological adoption, and policy enforcement.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the circular economy model with arrows showing reuse, recycle, and reduce principles.
articles.trivia: The concept of a circular economy is gaining traction globally as an alternative to the traditional linear 'take-make-dispose' model, aiming to keep resources in use for as long as possible.

articles.headline: India and Vietnam Strengthen Maritime Security Cooperation in South China Sea Context
articles.explainer:
• India and Vietnam held their annual bilateral dialogue on maritime security, emphasizing joint efforts to ensure peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing naval cooperation, joint exercises, information sharing, and capacity building for maritime surveillance.
• Both nations reiterated the importance of adhering to international law, including UNCLOS, in resolving disputes in the South China Sea.
• India offered further assistance to Vietnam in defence technology transfer and training, reinforcing their Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• The strengthened cooperation is seen as a significant step in countering growing geopolitical complexities and promoting regional security.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
keyTerms: UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea)
prelimsPoints: India and Vietnam held bilateral dialogue on maritime security.
Focus on Indo-Pacific peace and stability.
Emphasized adherence to UNCLOS for South China Sea disputes.
India offered defence technology transfer and training.
Part of India-Vietnam Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's growing maritime cooperation with Vietnam in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific security architecture.
Discuss how India's 'Act East Policy' is reflected in its defence and security engagements with Southeast Asian nations like Vietnam.
Evaluate the challenges posed by the South China Sea disputes to regional stability and the role of international law in their resolution.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Vietnam conducting a joint maritime exercise.
articles.trivia: India and Vietnam upgraded their relationship to a 'Comprehensive Strategic Partnership' in 2016, reflecting the deepening ties across various sectors.

articles.headline: National Digital Health Mission Expands Reach to Rural and Remote Areas
articles.explainer:
• The National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) announced its expanded outreach program, aiming to integrate rural and remote healthcare facilities into its digital framework.
• This phase focuses on providing digital health IDs to more citizens, enabling teleconsultations, and digitizing health records in underserved regions.
• Training programs for healthcare workers in remote areas are being ramped up to ensure effective adoption and utilization of digital tools.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the urban-rural health divide by improving accessibility, efficiency, and quality of healthcare services nationwide.
• Challenges include internet connectivity, digital literacy, and data privacy concerns, which the mission is actively addressing through various strategies.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
articles.source: National Health Authority / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: NDHM (now ABDM) expanding to rural/remote areas.
Focus on digital health IDs, teleconsultations, digitized records.
Training healthcare workers in remote regions.
Aims to bridge urban-rural health divide.
Addressing challenges like connectivity, digital literacy, data privacy.
mainsPoints: Assess the transformative potential of the National Digital Health Mission in improving healthcare delivery and access, especially in India's rural landscape.
Discuss the ethical and practical challenges associated with large-scale digitization of health records and the measures required to ensure data privacy and security.
Analyze how digital health initiatives can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and the Sustainable Development Goals related to health.
articles.imageDescription: A healthcare worker in a rural setting using a tablet for digital health services.
articles.trivia: The National Digital Health Mission was renamed to Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in September 2021, integrating it with the broader Ayushman Bharat framework.

articles.headline: India's Solar Manufacturing Sector Sees Significant Boost with PLI Scheme Success
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for High-Efficiency Solar PV Modules has significantly bolstered domestic manufacturing capabilities in India.
• Several new large-scale solar module and cell manufacturing units have commenced operations, reducing reliance on imports and boosting job creation.
• This surge in domestic production is crucial for India's ambitious renewable energy targets, including achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030.
• The scheme has attracted both domestic and international investors, fostering technological advancements and economies of scale in the sector.
• Challenges remain in securing raw material supply chains and ensuring competitive pricing against global manufacturers.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / Business Standard
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for High-Efficiency Solar PV Modules boosted domestic manufacturing.
New manufacturing units commenced operations.
Crucial for India's 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity target by 2030.
Attracted domestic and international investors.
Challenges: raw material supply chains, competitive pricing.
mainsPoints: Examine the efficacy of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in transforming India's manufacturing sector, with a specific focus on solar energy.
Discuss the role of domestic manufacturing in achieving India's renewable energy goals and enhancing energy security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India to become a global hub for solar equipment manufacturing, considering technological advancements and geopolitical factors.
articles.imageDescription: A solar panel manufacturing facility with workers assembling PV modules.
articles.trivia: India launched the National Solar Mission in 2010, one of the eight missions under the National Action Plan on Climate Change, to promote solar power development.

articles.headline: ASI Initiates Restoration and Conservation of Ancient Ganesha Idols in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has launched a special project for the restoration and conservation of several ancient Ganesha idols and sculptures in Odisha.
• Many of these idols, dating back to the medieval period, are found in various temples and archaeological sites, facing degradation due to natural elements and human activity.
• The project involves scientific cleaning, structural consolidation, and chemical preservation techniques to ensure their longevity.
• This initiative aims to protect Odisha's rich cultural heritage and promote archaeological tourism in the region.
• Community involvement and awareness programs are also part of the project to foster local stewardship of these historical artifacts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
articles.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Pioneer
prelimsPoints: ASI launched project for Ganesha idol restoration in Odisha.
Idols date back to the medieval period.
Involves scientific cleaning, structural consolidation, chemical preservation.
Aims to protect cultural heritage and promote tourism.
Includes community involvement and awareness programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving ancient religious sculptures and archaeological sites for understanding India's cultural and historical evolution.
Examine the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in conserving heritage sites across the country, including funding, expertise, and public awareness.
Suggest measures to enhance community participation and integrate cultural heritage preservation with sustainable tourism development.
articles.imageDescription: An ancient stone idol of Lord Ganesha undergoing restoration by ASI experts.
articles.trivia: Odisha is known for its rich temple architecture, particularly the Kalinga style, with many temples dedicated to various Hindu deities, including Ganesha.

articles.headline: New Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Launched to Combat Food Insecurity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new 'National Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture' to address the impacts of climate change on food security.
• The framework emphasizes developing and promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision farming techniques, and improved water management practices.
• It includes provisions for early warning systems for extreme weather events and climate-smart agricultural extension services for farmers.
• The initiative seeks to build the adaptive capacity of Indian agriculture against unpredictable weather patterns and ensure sustainable food production.
• Funding mechanisms and collaboration with research institutions are central to the successful implementation of this long-term strategy.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / DD News
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Agriculture launched 'National Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture'.
Focus on drought-resistant crops, precision farming, water management.
Includes early warning systems and climate-smart extension services.
Aims to build adaptive capacity against unpredictable weather.
Emphasizes funding and research collaboration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifarious challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and its implications for national food security.
Critically evaluate the key components of the National Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture and its potential to enhance farmers' resilience.
Suggest policy interventions and technological innovations required to transition Indian agriculture towards greater sustainability and climate adaptability.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting drought-resistant crops in a field, with modern irrigation systems.
articles.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture, with studies predicting significant yield reductions for major crops like wheat and rice under various warming scenarios.

articles.headline: Government Constitutes Expert Body for AI Ethics and Governance Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has established an expert advisory body to develop a comprehensive framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The body comprises leading AI researchers, legal experts, ethicists, and industry stakeholders, tasked with ensuring responsible and equitable AI development in India.
• Key focus areas include data privacy, algorithmic bias, accountability, and the societal impact of AI technologies across various sectors.
• This move reflects India's commitment to becoming a global leader in AI while addressing potential risks and promoting trust in AI systems.
• The framework is expected to guide policy-making, regulatory oversight, and industry best practices for AI applications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: MeitY constituted expert body for AI ethics and governance.
Body includes AI researchers, legal experts, ethicists, industry stakeholders.
Focus areas: data privacy, algorithmic bias, accountability, societal impact.
Aims for responsible and equitable AI development.
Framework to guide policy, regulation, industry best practices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical dilemmas and governance challenges posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence and its widespread applications.
Analyze the significance of establishing a national framework for AI ethics in India, considering its potential to foster innovation while mitigating risks.
Suggest key principles and regulatory mechanisms that should be incorporated into India's AI governance framework to ensure fairness, transparency, and accountability.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic representing artificial intelligence with interconnected nodes and data streams.
articles.trivia: India is among the top countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration and talent availability, making it a significant player in the future of AI development.

articles.headline: Enhanced Border Infrastructure Development in Northeast: Focus on Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced accelerated development of border infrastructure projects in India's Northeast region, particularly along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• The initiative focuses on improving road and rail connectivity, constructing strategic bridges, and upgrading surveillance systems to enhance border security and facilitate trade.
• This strategic push aims to bolster the region's economic integration with mainland India and its neighbors, reducing isolation and promoting inclusive growth.
• The projects are being implemented under the 'Vibrant Villages Programme' and other border area development schemes, ensuring benefits for local populations.
• Challenges include difficult terrain, adverse weather conditions, and geopolitical sensitivities, which require robust planning and execution.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: MHA announced accelerated border infrastructure development in Northeast.
Focus on road/rail connectivity, strategic bridges, surveillance systems.
Aims to enhance border security and trade.
Projects under 'Vibrant Villages Programme' and other schemes.
Challenges: difficult terrain, weather, geopolitical sensitivities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure in India's Northeast region for national security and regional development.
Discuss how improved connectivity and economic integration can address the challenges of isolation and underdevelopment in border areas.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like the 'Vibrant Villages Programme' in promoting inclusive growth and strengthening border management.
articles.imageDescription: Construction work on a strategic road in a mountainous border region of Northeast India.
articles.trivia: The 'Vibrant Villages Programme' was launched to enhance infrastructure and livelihood opportunities in border villages, encouraging residents to stay in their native places and strengthening border security.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision 2030' Strategy for Sustainable Tourism Revival
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched 'Vision 2030: A Strategy for Sustainable Tourism Revival and Growth', aiming to position India as a premier global tourist destination.
• The strategy focuses on promoting eco-tourism, cultural heritage tourism, and wellness tourism, with an emphasis on local community engagement and environmental preservation.
• Key initiatives include infrastructure upgrades, enhanced digital marketing, skill development for hospitality professionals, and simplified visa processes.
• It seeks to attract both domestic and international tourists, leveraging India's diverse offerings and unique cultural experiences.
• The plan also addresses challenges such as sustainable waste management in tourist areas and responsible tourism practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Investment models
Tourism sector
articles.source: Ministry of Tourism / Press Information Bureau
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism launched 'Vision 2030' for sustainable tourism.
Focus on eco-tourism, cultural heritage, wellness tourism.
Key initiatives: infrastructure, digital marketing, skill development, visa simplification.
Aims to attract domestic and international tourists.
Addresses waste management and responsible tourism.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of India's tourism sector to contribute to economic growth, employment generation, and cultural exchange.
Critically evaluate the 'Vision 2030' strategy for sustainable tourism, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses in addressing the sector's challenges.
Discuss the importance of responsible tourism practices and community involvement in ensuring the long-term sustainability of tourist destinations in India.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian tourist attractions like the Taj Mahal, beaches of Goa, and Himalayan landscapes.
articles.trivia: World Tourism Day is celebrated annually on September 27th, emphasizing the importance of tourism's social, cultural, political, and economic value.

articles.headline: Ministry Notifies Stricter E-Waste Management Rules, Emphasizing Extended Producer Responsibility
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified updated E-Waste Management Rules, introducing stricter norms for producers, recyclers, and consumers.
• The revised rules place greater emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), mandating higher collection targets and ensuring environmentally sound recycling practices.
• They aim to streamline the collection, dismantling, and recycling of electronic waste, minimizing its adverse environmental and health impacts.
• New provisions include a robust online portal for tracking e-waste movement and greater accountability for bulk consumers.
• The move is crucial for managing India's rapidly growing e-waste generation and promoting a circular economy approach in electronics.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: Updated E-Waste Management Rules notified by MoEF&CC.
Stricter norms, greater emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
Mandates higher collection targets and sound recycling.
Includes online portal for tracking e-waste.
Crucial for managing growing e-waste and promoting circular economy.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and its significance in tackling the challenges of e-waste management in India.
Critically analyze the provisions of the updated E-Waste Management Rules and their potential impact on producers, consumers, and recyclers.
Discuss the socio-economic and environmental implications of improper e-waste disposal and suggest measures to enhance public awareness and participation in e-waste recycling.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets representing e-waste, with a recycling symbol.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest e-waste generators globally, with a significant portion of it being handled by the informal sector, posing serious health and environmental risks.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Reviews Implementation Progress of National Education Policy 2020
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Education conducted a comprehensive review of the progress made in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The review focused on key reforms such as curriculum restructuring, integration of vocational education, teacher training, and the establishment of new regulatory bodies.
• While significant strides have been made in certain areas, challenges related to funding, infrastructure development, and faculty recruitment were highlighted.
• The committee emphasized the need for greater coordination between central and state governments to ensure uniform implementation across the country.
• Recommendations included accelerating the digitization of educational content and prioritizing early childhood care and education initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Committee reviewed NEP 2020 implementation.
Focus on curriculum, vocational education, teacher training, regulatory bodies.
Challenges: funding, infrastructure, faculty recruitment.
Called for better Centre-State coordination.
Recommendations: digitisation of content, ECCE priority.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reforming India's education system and addressing contemporary challenges.
Discuss the key challenges encountered during the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning federal structure, funding, and quality of education.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education for all, aligning with the vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the future of education.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century and replaced the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE) of 1986.

articles.headline: India Aims to Bolster Chip Design Ecosystem with New Incentives and Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new incentives and skill development programs to strengthen India's semiconductor chip design ecosystem.
• The initiative aims to attract global semiconductor majors to set up design and R&D centers in India and nurture domestic talent.
• Focus areas include providing financial support for startups, creating advanced design infrastructure, and establishing specialized academic courses.
• This strategic push is critical for achieving self-reliance in cutting-edge electronics and positioning India as a global hub for semiconductor innovation.
• Addressing intellectual property rights, access to advanced fabrication facilities, and global market linkages remain key challenges.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: MeitY announced incentives for India's chip design ecosystem.
Aims to attract global semiconductor majors and nurture domestic talent.
Focus on financial support for startups, design infrastructure, academic courses.
Critical for self-reliance in electronics and global semiconductor hub status.
Challenges: IPR, access to fabrication, market linkages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative for India to develop a robust semiconductor chip design and manufacturing ecosystem in the current geopolitical and technological landscape.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives and incentives in attracting investments and fostering innovation in India's semiconductor sector.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India to achieve self-reliance in semiconductor technology and its implications for national security and economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of a microchip with intricate circuitry, symbolizing semiconductor technology.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is highly concentrated, with a few countries dominating manufacturing, making self-reliance a critical goal for many nations including India.

articles.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Mission Launched to Tackle Extreme Weather Events
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Mission' to enhance preparedness and response to urban flooding.
• The mission focuses on integrated urban water management, upgrading drainage infrastructure, and implementing nature-based solutions for flood mitigation.
• It involves mapping vulnerable areas, developing early warning systems, and capacity building for urban local bodies and emergency services.
• The initiative is a direct response to the increasing frequency and intensity of extreme rainfall events and their devastating impact on Indian cities.
• Public awareness campaigns and community participation are integral components to build a resilient urban population.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / NDTV
prelimsPoints: MoHUA launched 'National Urban Flood Resilience Mission'.
Focus on urban water management, drainage upgrade, nature-based solutions.
Involves vulnerability mapping, early warning systems, capacity building.
Response to increasing extreme rainfall events.
Includes public awareness and community participation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the primary causes and consequences of urban flooding in Indian cities, particularly in the context of climate change and rapid urbanization.
Critically evaluate the key strategies and components of the National Urban Flood Resilience Mission and its potential to mitigate flood risks.
Suggest innovative approaches and policy reforms for sustainable urban planning and infrastructure development to build flood-resilient cities in India.
articles.imageDescription: A flooded city street with cars submerged, highlighting the impact of urban flooding.
articles.trivia: Chennai's 2015 floods were one of the most devastating urban flood events in recent Indian history, highlighting the urgent need for robust urban flood management strategies.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Role in Global Health Security Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India reaffirmed its commitment to playing an enhanced role in global health security initiatives, particularly in vaccine development, equitable access, and pandemic preparedness.
• At a virtual summit of global health leaders, India highlighted its 'pharmacy of the world' capabilities and its readiness to share expertise and resources.
• Discussions focused on strengthening global surveillance systems, rapid response mechanisms, and building resilient health supply chains.
• India emphasized the need for a more inclusive and equitable global health governance framework, advocating for greater representation of developing countries.
• This commitment aligns with India's foreign policy objectives of promoting multilateralism and addressing global challenges through collective action.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / WHO
prelimsPoints: India pledged enhanced role in global health security.
Highlighted 'pharmacy of the world' capabilities.
Focus on vaccine development, equitable access, pandemic preparedness.
Called for inclusive global health governance, more developing country representation.
Aligns with India's multilateral foreign policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and contemporary role as the 'pharmacy of the world' and its significance for global health security.
Analyze the challenges in achieving equitable access to vaccines and healthcare resources globally, and India's potential contributions to addressing these disparities.
Examine the need for reforms in global health governance institutions to ensure a more inclusive and effective response to future pandemics and health crises.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing a globe with a medical cross and Indian flag, symbolizing India's contribution to global health.
articles.trivia: India is home to a significant number of WHO prequalified vaccine manufacturers, supplying a large portion of global vaccine demand, especially for low and middle-income countries.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues New Directives for Cybersecurity of Critical Information Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued new, stringent directives for enhancing cybersecurity of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
• The directives mandate regular security audits, deployment of advanced threat detection systems, and strict incident response protocols for entities managing CII.
• This move aims to protect vital national assets, including power grids, financial systems, and telecommunication networks, from sophisticated cyber attacks.
• Compliance with these directives is crucial for ensuring national security and maintaining economic stability in an increasingly digitized world.
• Challenges include a shortage of skilled cybersecurity professionals and the evolving nature of cyber threats, necessitating continuous upgrades and training.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Cyber security
prelimsPoints: CERT-In issued new directives for Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) cybersecurity.
Mandates security audits, advanced threat detection, incident response protocols.
Aims to protect power grids, financial systems, telecom networks.
Crucial for national security and economic stability.
Challenges: skilled professionals shortage, evolving cyber threats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyber attacks on Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) and its potential implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Critically analyze the new directives issued by CERT-In for enhancing cybersecurity of CII and their effectiveness in building a robust cyber defense posture.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including policy, technology, and human resource development, to safeguard India's digital assets from advanced persistent threats.
articles.imageDescription: A padlock icon overlaid on a digital network, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: CERT-In operates under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and is the national agency for performing various functions in the area of cybersecurity incidents.

highlights: India-Africa Summit concludes with 'Delhi Declaration' on strategic cooperation, pledging $12 billion in credit to African nations.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains repo rate at 6.50% amidst persistent inflation concerns, particularly from food and crude oil.
Supreme Court adjourns hearing on review petitions challenging Electoral Bonds scheme, signaling potential for a larger bench review.
ISRO successfully test-fires a new semi-cryogenic engine for future heavy-lift rockets and Gaganyaan missions, boosting indigenous space capabilities.
Government launches 'National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management 2025-30' to reduce waste and promote resource utilization.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Delhi Declaration' recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It was adopted at the India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS-III).
2. It primarily focuses on defence cooperation and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.
3. India pledged $12 billion in concessional credit to African nations for infrastructure development.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The 'Delhi Declaration' was adopted at the India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS-III). Statement 2 is incorrect: While defence and maritime security are part of the cooperation, the declaration encompasses broader areas including trade, investment, climate change, and digital transformation, not primarily defence. Statement 3 is correct: India indeed pledged an additional $12 billion in concessional credit to African nations over the next five years, focusing on infrastructure and sustainable development projects.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary reason cited by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) for maintaining the benchmark repo rate at 6.50%?
options: (a) To boost export competitiveness in a global slowdown.
(b) To curb persistent inflationary pressures, especially from food prices.
(c) To encourage greater liquidity in the banking system.
(d) To support government borrowing at lower interest rates.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To curb persistent inflationary pressures, especially from food prices.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The RBI's MPC explicitly cited persistent inflationary concerns, particularly from food prices and global crude oil volatility, as the primary reason for holding the repo rate steady, aiming to achieve its medium-term inflation target of 4%.

prelimsMocks.question: The newly test-fired semi-cryogenic engine by ISRO uses which of the following as propellants?
prelimsMocks.explanation: ISRO's new semi-cryogenic engine is designed to use a combination of liquid oxygen and refined kerosene, specifically referred to as ISROSENE, as its propellants. Liquid Hydrogen and Liquid Oxygen are typically used in cryogenic engines, while solid propellants are used in solid rocket motors.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management 2025-30':
1. It was launched by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology.
2. Its primary goal is to maximize waste generation and promote incineration.
3. It focuses on developing robust infrastructure for waste segregation, collection, and processing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The plan was launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. Statement 2 is incorrect: Its primary goal is to drastically reduce waste generation and maximize resource utilization, promoting recycling and reuse, not maximizing waste or incineration. Statement 3 is correct: It focuses on developing robust infrastructure for waste segregation, collection, processing, and disposal.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the key focus of the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Mission' recently launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs?
options: (a) Developing satellite-based flood forecasting for rural areas only.
(b) Promoting only structural measures like dams and embankments.
(c) Integrated urban water management, upgrading drainage, and nature-based solutions.
(d) Relocating all urban populations from flood-prone zones.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Integrated urban water management, upgrading drainage, and nature-based solutions.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Urban Flood Resilience Mission focuses on integrated urban water management, upgrading drainage infrastructure, and implementing nature-based solutions for flood mitigation, along with early warning systems and capacity building for urban local bodies.

mainsMocks.question: The India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) has emerged as a crucial platform for South-South cooperation. Discuss the key outcomes of the recent IAFS-III, highlighting its significance for India's geopolitical and economic aspirations in the 21st century.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce IAFS as a platform for South-South cooperation. Detail the key outcomes of IAFS-III, such as the Delhi Declaration, Framework for Strategic Cooperation, and financial pledges. Analyze the significance for India's 'Global South' leadership, economic diplomacy, energy/resource security, counter-terrorism efforts, and balancing geopolitical influences. Conclude with the long-term vision of India-Africa partnership.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the challenges faced by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in balancing its mandates of inflation control and economic growth, particularly in the context of global uncertainties and domestic supply-side shocks. What role can fiscal policy play in complementing the RBI's efforts?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Government Budgeting; Monetary policy.
mainsMocks.approach: Explain the dual mandate of RBI (inflation targeting and growth support). Discuss challenges like imported inflation (crude oil), food price volatility, global supply chain disruptions, and geopolitical tensions. Analyze the limitations of monetary policy in addressing supply-side shocks. Detail how fiscal policy (e.g., targeted subsidies, infrastructure spending, tax reforms) can complement monetary policy to stabilize the economy and foster sustainable growth. Conclude on the need for coordinated monetary and fiscal strategies.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambition to become a global leader in space technology necessitates sustained innovation in propulsion systems. In this context, discuss the significance of ISRO's successful test-firing of the new semi-cryogenic engine and its implications for India's future space missions and strategic autonomy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Awareness in the fields of Space.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's space ambitions and the importance of propulsion technology. Explain what a semi-cryogenic engine is and its advantages (cost-effectiveness, power). Detail the significance of ISRO's recent test-firing for future missions like Reusable Launch Vehicles (RLV) and Gaganyaan. Discuss implications for strategic autonomy, reduced foreign dependence, commercial space market competitiveness, and fostering domestic scientific and technological capabilities. Conclude on India's trajectory in global space leadership.

quickBytes.text: November 26 is celebrated as Constitution Day (Samvidhan Divas) in India, marking the adoption of the Constitution in 1949.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is a policy approach under which producers are given a significant responsibility for the treatment or disposal of post-consumer products.
The 'Vibrant Villages Programme' aims to enhance infrastructure and livelihood opportunities in border villages to prevent out-migration.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a 6-member body in India, headed by the RBI Governor, responsible for determining the policy interest rates.
India is a founding member of the International Solar Alliance (ISA), an inter-governmental organization promoting solar energy.
The National Digital Health Mission (now Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission) aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
ISROSENE is a refined kerosene variant used by ISRO as fuel in its semi-cryogenic engines.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national nodal agency for responding to computer security incidents.
The Kalinga style of architecture is a prominent ancient Indian temple architecture style, primarily found in Odisha.
The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme is designed to boost domestic manufacturing and exports in various sectors.

GS1.headline: ASI Initiates Restoration and Conservation of Ancient Ganesha Idols in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has launched a special project for the restoration and conservation of several ancient Ganesha idols and sculptures in Odisha.
• Many of these idols, dating back to the medieval period, are found in various temples and archaeological sites, facing degradation due to natural elements and human activity.
• The project involves scientific cleaning, structural consolidation, and chemical preservation techniques to ensure their longevity.
• This initiative aims to protect Odisha's rich cultural heritage and promote archaeological tourism in the region.
• Community involvement and awareness programs are also part of the project to foster local stewardship of these historical artifacts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
GS1.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Pioneer
prelimsPoints: ASI launched project for Ganesha idol restoration in Odisha.
Idols date back to the medieval period.
Involves scientific cleaning, structural consolidation, chemical preservation.
Aims to protect cultural heritage and promote tourism.
Includes community involvement and awareness programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving ancient religious sculptures and archaeological sites for understanding India's cultural and historical evolution.
Examine the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in conserving heritage sites across the country, including funding, expertise, and public awareness.
Suggest measures to enhance community participation and integrate cultural heritage preservation with sustainable tourism development.
GS1.imageDescription: An ancient stone idol of Lord Ganesha undergoing restoration by ASI experts.
GS1.trivia: Odisha is known for its rich temple architecture, particularly the Kalinga style, with many temples dedicated to various Hindu deities, including Ganesha.

GS1.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Mission Launched to Tackle Extreme Weather Events
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Mission' to enhance preparedness and response to urban flooding.
• The mission focuses on integrated urban water management, upgrading drainage infrastructure, and implementing nature-based solutions for flood mitigation.
• It involves mapping vulnerable areas, developing early warning systems, and capacity building for urban local bodies and emergency services.
• The initiative is a direct response to the increasing frequency and intensity of extreme rainfall events and their devastating impact on Indian cities.
• Public awareness campaigns and community participation are integral components to build a resilient urban population.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management
GS1.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / NDTV
prelimsPoints: MoHUA launched 'National Urban Flood Resilience Mission'.
Focus on urban water management, drainage upgrade, nature-based solutions.
Involves vulnerability mapping, early warning systems, capacity building.
Response to increasing extreme rainfall events.
Includes public awareness and community participation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the primary causes and consequences of urban flooding in Indian cities, particularly in the context of climate change and rapid urbanization.
Critically evaluate the key strategies and components of the National Urban Flood Resilience Mission and its potential to mitigate flood risks.
Suggest innovative approaches and policy reforms for sustainable urban planning and infrastructure development to build flood-resilient cities in India.
GS1.imageDescription: A flooded city street with cars submerged, highlighting the impact of urban flooding.
GS1.trivia: Chennai's 2015 floods were one of the most devastating urban flood events in recent Indian history, highlighting the urgent need for robust urban flood management strategies.

GS2.headline: India-Africa Summit Concludes, Unveils Delhi Declaration on Strategic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The third India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS-III) concluded in New Delhi, adopting the 'Delhi Declaration' and a comprehensive 'Framework for Strategic Cooperation'.
• Key areas of collaboration include enhanced trade and investment, defence and security, climate change, digital transformation, and capacity building.
• India pledged an additional $12 billion in concessional credit to African nations over the next five years, focusing on infrastructure and sustainable development projects.
• The declaration emphasizes a shared vision for a multipolar world order, advocating for reforms in global institutions, including the UN Security Council.
• Leaders discussed joint efforts to combat terrorism, maritime piracy, and promote food and energy security across the continent.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: IAFS-III held in New Delhi.
Adopted 'Delhi Declaration' and 'Framework for Strategic Cooperation'.
India pledged $12 billion in concessional credit to Africa.
Focus on infrastructure, sustainable development, climate change, digital transformation.
Advocacy for UN Security Council reforms.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Delhi Declaration in strengthening India-Africa relations and its implications for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss how India's engagement with African nations aligns with its 'Global South' leadership aspirations and counter-balancing geopolitical influences.
Evaluate the potential economic and strategic benefits for both India and African countries through enhanced cooperation in defence, trade, and technology transfer.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders from India and various African nations shaking hands and posing for a group photograph at the India-Africa Forum Summit.
GS2.trivia: The first India-Africa Forum Summit was held in 2008, marking a significant step in India's structured engagement with the African continent.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Adjourns Hearing on Review Petitions for Electoral Bonds Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India adjourned the hearing on review petitions challenging its earlier judgment on the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Petitioners are seeking a re-examination of the constitutional validity of the scheme, citing concerns over transparency and potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• The earlier judgment had struck down the scheme, but the government had sought a review, arguing its importance for curbing black money in political funding.
• The court indicated that a larger bench might be constituted to address the complex constitutional questions involved.
• The outcome of these petitions will have significant implications for political funding and electoral transparency in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court adjourned hearing on Electoral Bonds review petitions.
Petitioners challenge constitutional validity, citing transparency concerns.
Government previously argued scheme curbs black money.
Potential for a larger bench to be constituted.
Impacts political funding and electoral transparency.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme in the context of electoral transparency and political funding reforms in India.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles related to free and fair elections, particularly concerning financial aspects of political parties.
Suggest alternative mechanisms for political funding that can ensure transparency, accountability, and reduce the influence of corporate donations.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The Electoral Bonds scheme was introduced in 2017 through a Finance Bill, amending several acts including the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

GS2.headline: India and Vietnam Strengthen Maritime Security Cooperation in South China Sea Context
GS2.explainer:
• India and Vietnam held their annual bilateral dialogue on maritime security, emphasizing joint efforts to ensure peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing naval cooperation, joint exercises, information sharing, and capacity building for maritime surveillance.
• Both nations reiterated the importance of adhering to international law, including UNCLOS, in resolving disputes in the South China Sea.
• India offered further assistance to Vietnam in defence technology transfer and training, reinforcing their Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• The strengthened cooperation is seen as a significant step in countering growing geopolitical complexities and promoting regional security.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
keyTerms: UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea)
prelimsPoints: India and Vietnam held bilateral dialogue on maritime security.
Focus on Indo-Pacific peace and stability.
Emphasized adherence to UNCLOS for South China Sea disputes.
India offered defence technology transfer and training.
Part of India-Vietnam Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's growing maritime cooperation with Vietnam in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific security architecture.
Discuss how India's 'Act East Policy' is reflected in its defence and security engagements with Southeast Asian nations like Vietnam.
Evaluate the challenges posed by the South China Sea disputes to regional stability and the role of international law in their resolution.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Vietnam conducting a joint maritime exercise.
GS2.trivia: India and Vietnam upgraded their relationship to a 'Comprehensive Strategic Partnership' in 2016, reflecting the deepening ties across various sectors.

GS2.headline: National Digital Health Mission Expands Reach to Rural and Remote Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) announced its expanded outreach program, aiming to integrate rural and remote healthcare facilities into its digital framework.
• This phase focuses on providing digital health IDs to more citizens, enabling teleconsultations, and digitizing health records in underserved regions.
• Training programs for healthcare workers in remote areas are being ramped up to ensure effective adoption and utilization of digital tools.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the urban-rural health divide by improving accessibility, efficiency, and quality of healthcare services nationwide.
• Challenges include internet connectivity, digital literacy, and data privacy concerns, which the mission is actively addressing through various strategies.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
GS2.source: National Health Authority / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: NDHM (now ABDM) expanding to rural/remote areas.
Focus on digital health IDs, teleconsultations, digitized records.
Training healthcare workers in remote regions.
Aims to bridge urban-rural health divide.
Addressing challenges like connectivity, digital literacy, data privacy.
mainsPoints: Assess the transformative potential of the National Digital Health Mission in improving healthcare delivery and access, especially in India's rural landscape.
Discuss the ethical and practical challenges associated with large-scale digitization of health records and the measures required to ensure data privacy and security.
Analyze how digital health initiatives can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and the Sustainable Development Goals related to health.
GS2.imageDescription: A healthcare worker in a rural setting using a tablet for digital health services.
GS2.trivia: The National Digital Health Mission was renamed to Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in September 2021, integrating it with the broader Ayushman Bharat framework.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Reviews Implementation Progress of National Education Policy 2020
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Education conducted a comprehensive review of the progress made in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The review focused on key reforms such as curriculum restructuring, integration of vocational education, teacher training, and the establishment of new regulatory bodies.
• While significant strides have been made in certain areas, challenges related to funding, infrastructure development, and faculty recruitment were highlighted.
• The committee emphasized the need for greater coordination between central and state governments to ensure uniform implementation across the country.
• Recommendations included accelerating the digitization of educational content and prioritizing early childhood care and education initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Committee reviewed NEP 2020 implementation.
Focus on curriculum, vocational education, teacher training, regulatory bodies.
Challenges: funding, infrastructure, faculty recruitment.
Called for better Centre-State coordination.
Recommendations: digitisation of content, ECCE priority.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reforming India's education system and addressing contemporary challenges.
Discuss the key challenges encountered during the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning federal structure, funding, and quality of education.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education for all, aligning with the vision of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the future of education.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century and replaced the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE) of 1986.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Role in Global Health Security Initiatives
GS2.explainer:
• India reaffirmed its commitment to playing an enhanced role in global health security initiatives, particularly in vaccine development, equitable access, and pandemic preparedness.
• At a virtual summit of global health leaders, India highlighted its 'pharmacy of the world' capabilities and its readiness to share expertise and resources.
• Discussions focused on strengthening global surveillance systems, rapid response mechanisms, and building resilient health supply chains.
• India emphasized the need for a more inclusive and equitable global health governance framework, advocating for greater representation of developing countries.
• This commitment aligns with India's foreign policy objectives of promoting multilateralism and addressing global challenges through collective action.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / WHO
prelimsPoints: India pledged enhanced role in global health security.
Highlighted 'pharmacy of the world' capabilities.
Focus on vaccine development, equitable access, pandemic preparedness.
Called for inclusive global health governance, more developing country representation.
Aligns with India's multilateral foreign policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and contemporary role as the 'pharmacy of the world' and its significance for global health security.
Analyze the challenges in achieving equitable access to vaccines and healthcare resources globally, and India's potential contributions to addressing these disparities.
Examine the need for reforms in global health governance institutions to ensure a more inclusive and effective response to future pandemics and health crises.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic showing a globe with a medical cross and Indian flag, symbolizing India's contribution to global health.
GS2.trivia: India is home to a significant number of WHO prequalified vaccine manufacturers, supplying a large portion of global vaccine demand, especially for low and middle-income countries.

GS3.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the benchmark repo rate at 6.50% in its latest review.
• This decision comes despite calls for a potential rate cut, as the MPC cited persistent inflationary concerns, especially from food prices and global crude oil volatility.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4% while supporting growth.
• Economic growth projections remain robust, but the central bank highlighted risks from geopolitical tensions and uneven monsoon distribution.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed, with the RBI indicating readiness to use various tools to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
GS3.source: Reserve Bank of India / Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.50%.
Decision by Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Primary reason: persistent inflation, particularly food prices and crude oil.
RBI's medium-term inflation target is 4%.
Geopolitical tensions and monsoon distribution cited as risks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, especially in the context of global uncertainties.
Discuss the effectiveness of the current monetary policy stance in addressing supply-side inflationary pressures and its impact on various sectors of the economy.
Examine the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy to achieve macroeconomic stability in India.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Test-Fires Next-Generation Semi-Cryogenic Engine
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful long-duration hot test of its new semi-cryogenic engine, powering future heavy-lift rockets.
• This engine, designed for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) missions and future Gaganyaan variants, uses a combination of liquid oxygen and refined kerosene (ISROSENE) as propellants.
• The test validates critical components and systems, moving India closer to developing more cost-effective and powerful launch capabilities.
• The indigenous development of such advanced propulsion systems is crucial for India's ambitions in human spaceflight and deep-space missions.
• This achievement reduces India's reliance on foreign technology and enhances its competitive edge in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully test-fired a new semi-cryogenic engine.
Engine uses liquid oxygen and ISROSENE (refined kerosene).
Designed for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) and Gaganyaan variants.
Aims for cost-effective and powerful launch capabilities.
Boosts India's human spaceflight and deep-space mission ambitions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous development of advanced propulsion systems like the semi-cryogenic engine for India's space program and national security.
Analyze how the development of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology can revolutionize space access and contribute to India's economic growth in the space sector.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in space technology, considering its current advancements and future aspirations.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic representation of ISRO's new semi-cryogenic engine undergoing a hot test.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first successful test of a cryogenic engine for its GSLV Mk-II rocket was in 2014, making India one of the few nations with this complex technology.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched a comprehensive 'National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management 2025-30'.
• The plan aims to drastically reduce waste generation, maximize resource utilization, and promote recycling and reuse across various sectors.
• It focuses on developing robust infrastructure for waste segregation, collection, processing, and disposal, with an emphasis on urban and industrial waste.
• Key initiatives include policy incentives for industries adopting circular economy principles, public awareness campaigns, and technological innovation in waste treatment.
• The plan is aligned with India's commitments to sustainable development goals and climate change mitigation, targeting a significant reduction in landfill burden.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / PIB
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Environment launched 'National Action Plan for Circular Economy in Waste Management 2025-30'.
Aims to reduce waste, maximize resource utilization, promote recycling/reuse.
Focus on urban and industrial waste infrastructure.
Includes policy incentives and public awareness campaigns.
Aligned with SDGs and climate change mitigation.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of a circular economy and its importance in addressing India's growing waste management challenges.
Analyze the key features and potential impacts of the National Action Plan on waste management, considering its economic, social, and environmental dimensions.
Discuss the challenges in implementing circular economy principles in India, particularly regarding behavioral change, technological adoption, and policy enforcement.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the circular economy model with arrows showing reuse, recycle, and reduce principles.
GS3.trivia: The concept of a circular economy is gaining traction globally as an alternative to the traditional linear 'take-make-dispose' model, aiming to keep resources in use for as long as possible.

GS3.headline: India's Solar Manufacturing Sector Sees Significant Boost with PLI Scheme Success
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for High-Efficiency Solar PV Modules has significantly bolstered domestic manufacturing capabilities in India.
• Several new large-scale solar module and cell manufacturing units have commenced operations, reducing reliance on imports and boosting job creation.
• This surge in domestic production is crucial for India's ambitious renewable energy targets, including achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030.
• The scheme has attracted both domestic and international investors, fostering technological advancements and economies of scale in the sector.
• Challenges remain in securing raw material supply chains and ensuring competitive pricing against global manufacturers.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / Business Standard
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for High-Efficiency Solar PV Modules boosted domestic manufacturing.
New manufacturing units commenced operations.
Crucial for India's 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity target by 2030.
Attracted domestic and international investors.
Challenges: raw material supply chains, competitive pricing.
mainsPoints: Examine the efficacy of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in transforming India's manufacturing sector, with a specific focus on solar energy.
Discuss the role of domestic manufacturing in achieving India's renewable energy goals and enhancing energy security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India to become a global hub for solar equipment manufacturing, considering technological advancements and geopolitical factors.
GS3.imageDescription: A solar panel manufacturing facility with workers assembling PV modules.
GS3.trivia: India launched the National Solar Mission in 2010, one of the eight missions under the National Action Plan on Climate Change, to promote solar power development.

GS3.headline: New Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Launched to Combat Food Insecurity
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new 'National Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture' to address the impacts of climate change on food security.
• The framework emphasizes developing and promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision farming techniques, and improved water management practices.
• It includes provisions for early warning systems for extreme weather events and climate-smart agricultural extension services for farmers.
